title: Introducing Quarters Theory (III) — Quarters Board
subtitle: Interactive Button-Controlled Quarter Level Management with Real-Time Trend Classification
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: LOW EXTRACT — UI/button management has no relevance to Python pipeline. One useful addition: SMA(50) trend classification added to the quarter level display. Quarters Theory trilogy complete; architecture fully understood after Parts 15-16.

core_concept.name: Interactive Quarters Board
core_concept.description: Extends Parts 15-16 with button-based chart controls. Toggles large quarters, small quarters, and overshoot zones on/off without editing source code. Adds SMA(50) trend classification (Uptrend/Downtrend/Sideways) alongside the quarter level display.
core_concept.new_vs_parts_15_16: Parts 15 and 16 had static bool inputs. Part 18 makes them interactive runtime toggles. No new calculation logic — same quarter level formulas. The only additive content is the SMA(50) trend label.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Minimal. We don't use MQL5 chart objects. The SMA trend classification is already subsumed by our StructureEngine (market_structure = BULLISH/BEARISH/RANGING is strictly better than SMA comparison).

trend_classification_addition.logic: if bid > sma50: 'Uptrend' elif bid < sma50: 'Downtrend' else: 'Sideways'
trend_classification_addition.weakness: SMA(50) lags significantly. Our StructureEngine HH/HL/LH/LL classification is structurally superior.

keep: SMA(50) as a minimal trend filter — confirms direction for quarter level trade decisions
Trend label alongside S/R levels — contextual framing for level interactions
discard: Interactive button UI — no relevance to Python pipeline
improve: Quarters trilogy is complete: combine Parts 15+16+18 into one QuarterLevelService class
Replace SMA trend with StructureEngine market_structure for level context

quarters_trilogy_summary.part_18: DISPLAY — UI toggles (discard for Python). SMA trend label = minor addition.
quarters_trilogy_summary.our_version: QuarterLevelService: compute levels + monitor intrusions + integrate with zone quality scoring. No UI layer.
